organizations.name: Youth Orchestra Los Angeles (YOLA)
organizations.blurb: The LA Phil's free music program: instruments, instruction, and orchestras for young musicians aged 6–18 across five LA County sites, including the Beckmen YOLA Center in Inglewood.

commitment.value: Several hours per week, after school

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a free, high-support program and live near a YOLA site.

ensembles.name: Beginning Orchestra
ensembles.desc: First-instrument and early ensemble training; no experience needed.

ensembles.name: Intermediate Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Full ensemble repertoire and sectionals.

ensembles.name: Advanced Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Flagship-level players; performances at the Hollywood Bowl and Walt Disney Concert Hall.

auditions.note: Start via the YOLA Interest Form; placement is by interview, not a formal audition. Space varies by site.
Advanced placement is audition-based; check the site for the current window.

sources.label: LA Phil — YOLA program & how to join

organizations.name: Colburn Community School of Performing Arts — Youth Orchestras
organizations.blurb: The Community School of Performing Arts at Colburn runs graded youth orchestras and a new advanced performance program feeding its renowned conservatory, in the Music Center district.
organizations.goodFit: Good fit for a serious, conservatory-track student; scholarships help with cost.

ensembles.name: Violin / Cello Ensembles
ensembles.desc: Entry-level ensembles building reading and ensemble fundamentals.

ensembles.name: String Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Intermediate/advanced strings; placement by audition.

ensembles.name: Colburn Youth Philharmonic
ensembles.desc: Top symphonic ensemble (replacing the Colburn Youth Orchestra for 2026–27).

ensembles.name: Advanced Music Performance Program
ensembles.desc: Highly selective Sunday program: orchestra, chamber music, theory, college-audition prep.

auditions.note: By application + video prescreen, then live audition (most recent round was held June 6–7, 2026). Check the site for the next window.
Advanced Music Performance Program — selective audition; one day a week (Sundays).

organizations.name: Los Angeles Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: A welcoming youth orchestra rehearsing Sunday afternoons in Encino, with an intermediate and an advanced ensemble and two concerts each semester.

season.value: 2026–27 season

rehearsal.value: Sunday afternoons, 1:30–5:00 PM (Encino)

commitment.value: Weekly rehearsals; two fall and two spring concerts

tuition.value: $750 per semester (or $1,400 paid in full for the year)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for an intermediate-to-advanced player (about two+ years of lessons) wanting a friendly Sunday orchestra in the Valley.

ensembles.name: Ruth Borun Concert Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Intermediate ensemble.

ensembles.name: Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Advanced ensemble; higher expectations for tone, dynamics, and musicianship.

window.value: August 22 & 23, 2026 (First Presbyterian Church, Encino)

auditions.note: For the Fall 2026 semester. Major & minor scales, LAYO-provided excerpts, and sight-reading. $100 audition fee is applied to tuition on acceptance.

organizations.name: Pasadena Youth Symphony Orchestras
organizations.blurb: The Pasadena Symphony Association's youth program: seven ensembles for grades 4–12, serving 400+ students from across Southern California, with weekly rehearsals and at least two concerts a year.

rehearsal.value: Weekly throughout the season

commitment.value: Weekly; students perform in at least two concerts each year

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for San Gabriel Valley families wanting a structured, multi-tier path from beginner to youth symphony.

ensembles.name: Beginning Strings
ensembles.desc: Entry ensemble — one of seven graded PYSO ensembles.

ensembles.name: Intermediate Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Strings, winds, and brass together.

ensembles.name: Youth Symphony
ensembles.desc: Top ensemble in the seven-tier program.

auditions.note: 2026–27 applications open in early April; placement by audition across seven ensembles. Check the site for in-person dates.

organizations.name: Glendale Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: An advanced, audition-based youth orchestra founded in 1989, performing three concerts each season at the Alex Theatre, with a members' concerto competition.

tuition.value: $875 per year (includes 5 concert tickets per concert)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for an advanced Glendale-area player who wants symphonic repertoire and a concerto-competition opportunity.

ensembles.name: Glendale Youth Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Single full orchestra; classical, film, and world music.

auditions.note: By appointment, typically late August before the season. Two prepared excerpts + two scales; merit-based. Check the site for the current date.

organizations.name: Santa Monica Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: A tuition-free, audition-free Sunday music program (part of Sounds Like LA) for ages 6–18, with three orchestra levels and a culturally diverse repertoire, rehearsing at Virginia Avenue Park.

rehearsal.value: Sunday afternoons, roughly 30 weeks Sept–June (Virginia Avenue Park)

commitment.value: Weekly (Sundays); open to any student who can commit to rehearsals

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for westside and coastal families wanting a free, welcoming first orchestra with no audition.

ensembles.name: Prelude Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Beginning level.

ensembles.name: Youth Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Intermediate level.

ensembles.name: Symphonic Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Most advanced of the three levels; opens for the Westside Chamber Orchestra.

window.value: Open enrollment — no audition; register each season

auditions.note: Audition-free; students are placed by level. Registration opens each season.

organizations.name: Los Angeles Philharmonic
organizations.blurb: One of the world's leading orchestras, resident at Walt Disney Concert Hall and the Hollywood Bowl.

commitment.value: Full-time professional

organizations.goodFit: For professional musicians auditioning for posted vacancies; for families, a world-class concert destination.

ensembles.name: Los Angeles Philharmonic
ensembles.desc: Full professional symphony orchestra.

auditions.note: Union audition process; sealed-round excerpts. See the LA Phil site for current openings.

organizations.name: Los Angeles Chamber Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Founded in 1968, a preeminent professional chamber orchestra performing orchestral, chamber, and Baroque programs across greater Los Angeles, led by Music Director Jaime Martín.

season.value: 2026/27 season

commitment.value: Full-time professional

organizations.goodFit: For professional players; for families, intimate, high-craft chamber concerts in DTLA and Beverly Hills.

ensembles.name: Los Angeles Chamber Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Professional chamber-scale orchestra.

performances.title: MOVEMENT(S)!
performances.venue: Walt Disney Concert Hall (DTLA) & Wallis Annenberg Center (Beverly Hills)
performances.program: Kodály; Carlos Simon (world premiere); Gabriela Lena Frank; de Falla

performances.title: Brahms, Beethoven, Ravaei (season finale)
performances.program: Brahms Violin Concerto (James Ehnes); Beethoven Symphony No. 7; world premiere by Kian Ravaei

auditions.note: Excerpts + chamber round. See the LACO site for current openings.

organizations.name: Pasadena Symphony and POPS
organizations.blurb: The San Gabriel Valley's professional orchestra: a classical season (Oct–May) at Ambassador Auditorium and a summer POPS series at the LA County Arboretum.

season.value: Classical season Oct–May at Ambassador Auditorium; POPS Jun–Sep at the LA County Arboretum

commitment.value: Per-service professional

organizations.goodFit: For professional players seeking section/sub work; for families, a regional classical and POPS concert series.

ensembles.name: Pasadena Symphony
ensembles.desc: Professional regional orchestra.

auditions.note: Resume + excerpts by posted vacancy.

organizations.name: Long Beach Symphony
organizations.blurb: Long Beach's professional orchestra since 1934, led by Music Director Eckart Preu, with a five-concert Classical Series at the Terrace Theater plus a POPS series and youth concerts.

season.value: 2026–27 Classical Series — five concerts at the Terrace Theater

commitment.value: Per-service professional

organizations.goodFit: For professional players; for families, a relaxed Classical Series in Long Beach with free pre-concert talks.

ensembles.name: Long Beach Symphony
ensembles.desc: Professional orchestra.

performances.title: Faces of America at 250
performances.program: Copland Fanfare for the Common Man; James P. Johnson Harlem Symphony; Gershwin Strike Up the Band

performances.title: Entangled in Passion
performances.program: Ravel Boléro; R. Strauss Don Juan; Rachmaninoff Piano Concerto No. 2 (Michelle Cann)

performances.title: Heaven & Earth
performances.program: Gabriela Lena Frank; Brahms Violin Concerto (Randall Goosby); Mozart Symphony No. 41 'Jupiter'

auditions.note: Union audition; excerpts provided by posted vacancy.

organizations.name: Culver City Symphony Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Founded in 1964 (originally the Westchester String Symphony), an orchestra that pairs professional players with talented amateurs and students, presenting free concerts in Culver City and a summer series as the Marina del Rey Summer Symphony.

season.value: 3–4 free concerts each season in Culver City; summers as the Marina del Rey Summer Symphony at Burton Chace Park

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for adult amateurs and advanced students who want to play alongside professionals, or for families wanting free local concerts.

ensembles.name: Culver City Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Mixed professional/amateur/student orchestra; becomes the Marina del Rey Summer Symphony in summer.

auditions.note: Includes professional, amateur, and student players; contact the orchestra to inquire about joining.

organizations.name: Inner City Youth Orchestra of Los Angeles
organizations.blurb: Tuition-free, no-audition orchestra of roughly 125 young musicians rooted in South Los Angeles, widely described as the largest majority-Black youth orchestra in the country. Players rehearse weekly alongside professional mentor-artists and close each season on the Walt Disney Concert Hall stage.

season.value: 2025–26 concert season (theme: 'I, Too, Sing America'; season finale July 12, 2026)

commitment.value: Weekly rehearsals; 12–15 concerts annually

tuition.value: Free — no tuition, no auditions, no competition for seats

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a tuition-free, no-audition orchestra where beginners and advanced players grow side by side with professional mentors.

ensembles.name: Inner City Youth Orchestra of Los Angeles
ensembles.desc: Single mixed-level orchestra of approximately 125 members; advanced players mentor newer ones and professional mentor-artists rehearse and perform alongside students.

auditions.instruments: All orchestral instruments — all skill levels welcome

window.value: Open enrollment — no audition; join any time via the interest form

auditions.note: No auditions — families submit an interest form and staff follow up; instruments can be provided if needed.

organizations.name: Claremont Young Musicians Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Auditioned youth orchestra program founded in 1989, with three Sunday-rehearsal ensembles running from beginning strings through a full symphony that has performed at Walt Disney Concert Hall and toured Europe. A Los Angeles Philharmonic member appears as soloist with the orchestra each January.

season.value: 2026–27 season

rehearsal.value: Sundays at Claremont High School, September through May: Overture Strings 2:00–3:00 PM, Intermezzo Orchestra 3:30–5:30 PM, CYMO 6:30–9:15 PM

commitment.value: Weekly Sunday rehearsals, September through May

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if your player wants a classic auditioned youth-symphony ladder in the east San Gabriel Valley with serious repertoire and LA Phil connections.

ensembles.name: Claremont Young Musicians Overture String Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Beginning orchestral experience for string players in grades 3–8, preparing them for the Intermezzo Orchestra (about 35 members).

ensembles.name: Claremont Young Musicians Intermezzo Orchestra
ensembles.desc: 75-member preparatory orchestra for intermediate players, ages 9–18, feeding into CYMO.

ensembles.name: Claremont Young Musicians Orchestra (CYMO)
ensembles.desc: Flagship symphony of musicians ages 12–20 performing major symphonic works at Bridges Hall of Music, Pomona College.

auditions.note: 2026–27 audition dates and excerpts to be available in July 2026; join the audition list via the application form.
2026–27 audition dates and excerpts to be available in July 2026; join the audition list via the application form.

window.value: September, two weeks after the CYMO and Intermezzo auditions

organizations.name: Santa Clarita Valley Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Santa Clarita's youth orchestra for nearly 40 years, with a beginner-to-advanced ensemble ladder and unusually adventurous programming that pairs classical repertoire with dancers, animators and cross-cultural works. Students can also join honors chamber music, a conducting program and travel opportunities.

season.value: Fall 2026 (semester-based; new members register by August 15, 2026)

rehearsal.value: Symphony Orchestra: Sundays 1:00–2:30 PM, Rancho Pico Jr. High School band room

commitment.value: Semester-based enrollment; all students except continuing Prelude members re-audition each fall

tuition.value: $420 per-semester ensemble fee (Symphony Orchestra, Spring 2026) plus $13 online processing fee; 10% sibling discount; payment plans available

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a friendly, community-minded orchestra in the Santa Clarita Valley that welcomes players from their first year through advanced repertoire.

ensembles.name: Prelude Ensemble
ensembles.desc: Entry-level ensemble for musicians with about one year of experience on any string, wind or brass instrument (including piano and percussion); continuing students do not re-audition.

ensembles.name: Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Mixed-level intermediate orchestra (typically 3+ years of experience) that prepares musicians for Novae Sinfonia.

ensembles.name: Novae Sinfonia
ensembles.desc: Most advanced orchestral ensemble; typical members have at least five years of experience.

ensembles.name: Honors Chamber Music
ensembles.desc: Advanced chamber program; members must be concurrently enrolled in Novae Sinfonia.

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion, piano (Novae Sinfonia)

window.value: Live auditions July 25, 2026 (returning students) and August 15, 2026 (new students) at Rancho Pico Jr. High School; digital video auditions accepted in advance

auditions.note: Scales, a solo piece and short orchestral excerpts; all new and returning students re-audition each fall.

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion, piano (Symphony Orchestra)

window.value: Live auditions July 25, 2026 (returning students) and August 15, 2026 (new students) at Rancho Pico Jr. High School; digital video auditions accepted in advance

auditions.note: Scales, a solo piece and short orchestral excerpts (excerpts updated 6/16/26).

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, piano, percussion (Prelude Ensemble)

window.value: New students register for Fall 2026 by August 15, 2026

auditions.note: New students demonstrate one year of experience, music reading and a one-octave scale; continuing Prelude students are exempt from re-auditioning.

organizations.name: CSUN Youth Orchestras
organizations.blurb: Multi-orchestra program for school-age musicians in residence at Cal State Northridge since the mid-1970s, serving the San Fernando Valley with three ensembles from intermediate strings up to the Youth Philharmonic. The program traces its roots to the Valley Young People's Orchestra founded in 1970.

season.value: 2025–2026 (first rehearsals September 13–14, 2025)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a university-hosted youth orchestra ladder in the San Fernando Valley with a competitive top ensemble.

ensembles.name: Camerata Strings
ensembles.desc: Intermediate string orchestra teaching the skills needed for successful orchestral playing.

ensembles.name: Symphony
ensembles.desc: Intermediate orchestra of 70–90 members playing standard works and arrangements; players expected to have roughly four or more years of experience.

ensembles.name: Philharmonic
ensembles.desc: Top-level orchestra of 80–100 students playing standard literature, mainly late-romantic and 20th-century; auditions are highly competitive.

window.value: Late spring each year

auditions.note: Site's posted registration details are from the 2025–26 cycle (video auditions were due before September 6, 2025); confirm current-year dates with the program.

window.value: Summer through early September each year

auditions.note: Annual recurring window per the ensembles page; 2026–27 specific dates not yet posted as of 2026-07-06 — the site's audition page still shows the 2025–26 cycle. Returning members re-register without re-auditioning.

sources.label: CSUN Youth Orchestras home (2025–26 audition info)

organizations.name: Claremont Symphony Orchestra
organizations.blurb: All-volunteer community symphony entering its 74th season, performing free concerts at Bridges Hall of Music on the Pomona College campus. The orchestra's association also sponsors the Claremont Youth Symphony Orchestra.

season.value: 2026–2027 (74th season); dates and programming to be posted

rehearsal.value: Monday evenings 7:30–10:00 PM at Bryant Rehearsal Hall, Thatcher Music Building, Pomona College

commitment.value: Volunteer membership; attendance at weekly rehearsals expected

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you're an adult amateur player near the east San Gabriel Valley looking for a welcoming volunteer symphony — or a family wanting free classical concerts.

ensembles.name: Claremont Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: All-volunteer community symphony led by Music Director Robert Sage; membership by audition.

window.value: Year-round, on an ongoing basis

auditions.note: String players contact the concertmaster; all other instruments contact the orchestra's audition coordinator. Confirm you can attend Monday rehearsals before requesting an audition.

organizations.name: Musique Sur La Mer Youth Orchestras
organizations.blurb: Long Beach youth orchestra family founded in 2000 by Marcy Sudock, running from beginner strings and a youth mariachi ensemble up to a touring youth symphony that has played the Sydney Opera House and Vienna's Musikverein. Rehearsals happen at the group's own waterfront studio at Shoreline Village.

season.value: Concert season runs late August through May, with four major concerts per season

rehearsal.value: At the Musique Sur La Mer studio, 423 Shoreline Village Drive, Suite CD, Long Beach

commitment.value: Four major concerts per season (late August–May) plus community performances; school music program participation (where available) and private lessons required for youth members

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if your Long Beach-area player wants a smaller, studio-based program with big travel ambitions, from first orchestra experience to international touring.

ensembles.name: La Petite Musique Training Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Entry ensemble for string players (grades 3–8) with about two years of lessons; no audition — seating assessments happen in the first two weeks of class.

ensembles.name: Shoreline Strings
ensembles.desc: Auditioned ensemble for strings, piano and percussion, grades 5–9, requiring developed vibrato and two-octave scales.

ensembles.name: Musique Sur La Mer Youth Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Advanced full orchestra (strings including harp, woodwinds, brass, piano and percussion) for ages 12–18, grades 7–12; performs locally and on international tours.

ensembles.name: Musique Sur La Mer Youth Honors Chamber Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Invitation-only chamber orchestra for advanced strings, woodwinds and piano concurrently enrolled in the Youth Symphony.

ensembles.name: Long Beach Offshore Winds Ensemble
ensembles.desc: Auditioned wind ensemble for advanced woodwinds, brass and percussion (grades 6–12 per the program page) performing symphonic band, jazz and chamber music; concurrent Youth Symphony membership required.

ensembles.name: Long Beach Youth Mariachis — Musica de la Playa
ensembles.desc: Open-enrollment mariachi ensemble for elementary through high school musicians with two years of lessons; no audition required.

auditions.instruments: Strings, woodwinds, brass, percussion, piano, harp (Youth Symphony Orchestra and Honors Chamber Orchestra)
auditions.note: Auditions are by scheduled appointment at the MSLM studio; the posted FAQ dates are from a prior season, so contact the office for the current schedule.

window.value: No audition — seating assessments during the first two weeks of class

auditions.note: No audition required; individual seating placement assessments take place during the first two weeks of class.

sources.label: Youth Programs (ensembles, financial aid and 2025–26 registration packet)
Audition, eligibility and FAQ (scholarships, season, rehearsal location)
Events calendar (Costa Rica concert tour, June 21–29, 2026)

organizations.name: Pacific Symphony
organizations.blurb: Orange County's resident professional orchestra performs classical, pops, family, and summer series at the Renée and Henry Segerstrom Concert Hall in Costa Mesa and Great Park Live in Irvine, with Artistic and Music Director Designate Alexander Shelley taking the podium.

season.value: 2026–27 season (concerts listed September 2026 through June 2027)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want to hear a professional orchestra close to home — or show your teen where serious training can lead.

ensembles.name: Pacific Symphony
ensembles.desc: The professional orchestra, performing classical, pops, family, and summer outdoor series across Orange County.

performances.title: Violin Virtuoso Joshua Bell & Alexander Shelley
performances.venue: Renée and Henry Segerstrom Concert Hall

performances.title: Shelley Conducts Mahler 2: Resurrection
performances.venue: Renée and Henry Segerstrom Concert Hall

window.value: Application deadline September 4, 2026; live auditions September 25–26, 2026

auditions.note: Online application plus $100 refundable deposit check mailed to the Irvine office.

window.value: Application deadline September 7, 2026; live auditions September 28–29, 2026
Application deadline October 5, 2026; live auditions October 19–20, 2026

auditions.note: Excerpt list marked 'coming soon' on the auditions page.

organizations.name: Pacific Symphony Youth Ensembles
organizations.blurb: Pacific Symphony's pre-professional training program for musicians in grades 6–12, with four ensembles that rehearse weekly at UC Irvine, receive coaching from Pacific Symphony musicians, and perform free concerts at the Renée and Henry Segerstrom Concert Hall.

rehearsal.value: Weekly, September through May, at UC Irvine: Youth Wind Ensemble & Youth Orchestra Sundays 1–4 PM; Youth Concert Band & Santiago Strings Sundays 5–7 PM

commitment.value: Members must actively participate in their school music program and study with a private teacher for the duration of the season; all members re-audition each year

tuition.value: $1,250 per season (Santiago Strings, Youth Concert Band); $1,500 per season (Youth Orchestra, Youth Wind Ensemble)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if your grade 6–12 musician wants conservatory-style training, coaching from professional players, and the chance to perform in a world-class hall.

ensembles.name: Pacific Symphony Santiago Strings
ensembles.desc: String orchestra for grades 6–9 (age 10+), led by Dr. Johanna Gamboa-Kroesen; violin, viola, cello, and double bass.

ensembles.name: Pacific Symphony Youth Concert Band
ensembles.desc: Concert band for woodwind, brass, and percussion players in grades 6–9 (age 10+), led by Angela Woo.

ensembles.name: Pacific Symphony Youth Wind Ensemble
ensembles.desc: Wind symphony for woodwind, brass, and percussion players in grades 9–12 (age 13+), led by Dr. Gregory X. Whitmore.

ensembles.name: Pacific Symphony Youth Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Full symphony orchestra of 100+ string, wind, and percussion players in grades 9–12 (age 13+), led by Pacific Symphony Assistant Conductor Pola Benke.

auditions.instruments: Strings (Santiago Strings); winds, brass, percussion (Youth Concert Band)

window.value: Held each spring for the following season; application generally available early March through early May (2026–27 applications closed May 1, 2026)

auditions.note: Video audition via Acceptd; $45 non-refundable fee (fee waivers available through Acceptd).

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion, harp, piano (Youth Orchestra); winds, brass, percussion, harp, piano (Youth Wind Ensemble)

window.value: Held each spring for the following season; application generally available early March through early May (2026–27 applications closed May 1, 2026)

auditions.note: All members re-audition annually; grade and age eligibility measured as of September 1 of the season year.

organizations.name: Orange County Youth Symphony and String Ensemble
organizations.blurb: Founded in 1970 and run by the Philharmonic Society of Orange County, the county's official youth orchestra pairs an advanced full symphony (OCYS) with an intermediate string ensemble (OCYSE), rehearsing Sunday afternoons at Irvine Valley College and performing in halls like the Segerstrom Concert Hall and Soka Performing Arts Center.

season.value: 2026–27 season runs late August 2026 through mid-May 2027

rehearsal.value: Sunday afternoons, 1:00–3:30 PM, Irvine Valley College Music & Dance Building

commitment.value: Active participation in your school music program is expected (verified with teachers each semester); three major concerts per season, with OCYS adding winter community concerts and a fall retreat

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if your advanced high schooler wants a full-orchestra challenge with retreats and a concerto competition, or your middle schooler is ready for a step up in strings.

ensembles.name: Orange County Youth Symphony (OCYS)
ensembles.desc: Full orchestra for advanced woodwind, brass, string, and percussion musicians in grades 9–13, led by Music Director Johannes Müller Stosch.

ensembles.name: Orange County Youth String Ensemble (OCYSE)
ensembles.desc: String ensemble for intermediate string players in grades 6–9, led by Conductor Lucy Lu, with sectionals and a chamber group program.

auditions.instruments: OCYS: violin, viola, cello, double bass, flute/piccolo, oboe/English horn, clarinet/bass clarinet, bassoon, French horn, trumpet, trombone, bass trombone, euphonium and tuba, percussion; harp and piano as needed
auditions.note: 2026–27 season auditions (opened March 1, 2026) closed May 13, 2026 with no extensions; $40 fee and video audition; next cycle not yet announced.

auditions.instruments: OCYSE: strings only (violin, viola, cello, double bass)
auditions.note: 2026–27 auditions closed May 13, 2026; in-person OCYSE callbacks planned for late August 2026 per the official page; next cycle not yet announced.

sources.label: OCYS/E audition information, tuition, schedule FAQ

organizations.name: South Coast Youth Symphony Orchestra
organizations.blurb: A registration-based youth orchestra founded in 2001 that welcomes players of all abilities, with a choice of weekly rehearsal sites in Mission Viejo, Garden Grove, and Ontario, monthly combined workshops, and a history of international touring — including a Carnegie Hall invitational tour planned for summer 2027.

season.value: 2026–2027 season

rehearsal.value: Weekly by location: Ontario Mondays 5–7 PM; Garden Grove Tuesdays 7–9 PM; Mission Viejo Wednesdays 7–9 PM; plus Saturday combined workshops at La Paz Intermediate School, Mission Viejo

commitment.value: Previous training on your instrument and enrollment in your school instrumental music program (if available) are required; registration includes a season obligation and commitment form

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a welcoming orchestra that takes all ability levels and lets you pick the rehearsal night and location that suits your family.

ensembles.name: South Coast Youth Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: One orchestra program open to musicians of all abilities, rehearsing at three sites (Mission Viejo, Garden Grove, Ontario) that combine monthly for workshops and perform together.

window.value: Registration for the 2026–27 season is open; registration and seating-placement audition videos accepted beginning May 25, 2026

auditions.note: Open to all abilities — the video audition is for seating placement only, and you can register before receiving audition results. Late/mid-season joining by phone or email.

organizations.name: Community Youth Orchestra of Southern California
organizations.blurb: A nonprofit founded in 1990 that serves students of all ages and ability levels — preschool through college — placing them by audition into one of four ensembles, with Friday evening rehearsals in the Irvine/Tustin area, three concerts a season, and economical tuition.

season.value: 2026–2027 season; rehearsals begin September 11, 2026

rehearsal.value: Once a week on Friday evenings, one to two hours depending on ensemble; 2026–27 rehearsal space not yet confirmed but will be in the Irvine/Tustin area

commitment.value: Signed attendance and behavior contract; students who miss more than 5 rehearsals during the season cannot play concerts and risk removal from their ensemble

tuition.value: 2026–27: $875 per student ($850 if paying by check/Zelle); 10% sibling discount; 20% discount for qualifying viola and brass students

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want an affordable, community-minded orchestra home for a beginner through advanced player of almost any age.

ensembles.name: Sinfonietta
ensembles.desc: Entry ensemble; students are placed by teachers based on age group and playing level.

ensembles.name: Philharmonia
ensembles.desc: Intermediate ensemble (described as the String Ensemble in program text).

ensembles.name: Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: The organization's top full-orchestra ensemble.

ensembles.name: Solisti
ensembles.desc: The newest ensemble in the four-ensemble lineup.

auditions.instruments: Orchestral strings, winds, and brass per the audition guide; harp listed among accepted instruments

window.value: 2026–27 sign-ups open now; prescreening video due by August 16, 2026; in-person auditions August 25–28, 2026; results emailed the first week of September

auditions.note: All ability levels accepted; the audition assigns students to the ensemble matching their age and level. $40 audition fee; all students including returners re-audition. Late joiners sometimes accommodated mid-season.

sources.label: CYOSC home (2026–27 sign-ups, summer camp, audition dates)

organizations.name: San Diego Symphony
organizations.blurb: San Diego's professional orchestra under music director Rafael Payare, performing downtown at the Jacobs Music Center and outdoors at The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park.

season.value: 2026–27 season at Jacobs Music Center, plus a summer season at The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

commitment.value: Full-time professional: 42-week contract (37 playing weeks plus 5 vacation weeks) for the 2026–27 season

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want to take the family to hear professionals live — this is a professional orchestra, not an enrollment program for kids.

ensembles.name: San Diego Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Full-time professional symphony orchestra performing at the Jacobs Music Center and The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park.

performances.title: Beethoven by the Bay
performances.venue: The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

performances.title: Distant Worlds: music from FINAL FANTASY
performances.venue: The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

performances.title: The Music of John Williams
performances.venue: The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

window.value: Applications due August 28, 2026; auditions October 5–6, 2026

auditions.note: Employment intended to begin no later than January 2027; refundable audition deposit required.

window.value: Applications due October 2, 2026; auditions November 7–9, 2026

auditions.note: Full repertoire list to be posted; all candidates with completed applications receive an audition time.

sources.label: San Diego Symphony home (2026–27 season nav + July 2026 concert listings)
Auditions — San Diego Symphony Orchestra (vacancies, CBA terms)

organizations.name: San Diego Youth Symphony and Conservatory
organizations.blurb: Founded in 1945 and based in Balboa Park, SDYS runs 13+ orchestras and wind ensembles with a clear ladder from first-time beginners to an advanced Symphony Orchestra, and tuition assistance for families who need it.

season.value: 2026–27 season; new-student registration re-opens July 6 and ends July 30, 2026, with auditions closing July 31, 2026

rehearsal.value: Weekly weekend rehearsals by ensemble — Saturdays or Sundays at Balboa Park or Liberty Station (e.g. Symphony Orchestra: Sundays 3:30–6:30 PM)

commitment.value: Full-season commitment: all students, including marching band students, join in the fall — no mid-year entry

tuition.value: $900–$1,450 per season depending on ensemble (Beginning ensembles $900; Symphony Orchestra $1,450)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a large, structured program where any level from age 7 to 18 can find a seat and move up year by year.

ensembles.name: Beginning Strings / Winds / Brass / Percussion
ensembles.desc: No-experience-required entry ensembles at Liberty Station; no audition needed.

ensembles.name: Debut Strings & Debut Winds
ensembles.desc: First performing ensembles for students with a little experience, rehearsing in Balboa Park.

ensembles.name: Overture Strings & Overture Winds
ensembles.desc: Late-beginner ensembles building technical and collaborative skills.

ensembles.name: Symphonic Strings
ensembles.desc: Early-intermediate string orchestra.

ensembles.name: Concert Strings, Concert Winds & Philharmonia
ensembles.desc: Early-to-late-intermediate orchestras and winds for grades 5–12.

ensembles.name: Sinfonia & Wind Symphony
ensembles.desc: Late-intermediate full orchestra and wind symphony for grades 7–12.

ensembles.name: Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Flagship advanced orchestra (grades 9–12) playing professional-level repertoire under Artistic Director Sameer Patel.

auditions.instruments: Select instruments: viola, double bass, bass clarinet, bassoon, saxophones, horn, trumpet, trombone, bass trombone, euphonium, tuba, percussion

window.value: Register July 6–30, 2026 ($50 non-refundable fee); video auditions due July 31, 2026

auditions.note: One audition places students across all ensembles; percussion auditions in person Thursday, August 13; results emailed around the first two weeks of August.

window.value: Registration open through July 31, 2026

auditions.note: No audition required for beginning ensembles — register and pay the registration fee.

sources.label: How To Join (2026–27 registration/audition deadlines, tuition assistance, FAQ)
Who We Are (13+ ensembles with ages, tuition, rehearsal times)
Legacy SDYS site (founded 1945, 6th oldest US youth symphony; page content stale)

organizations.name: San Diego Civic Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Seven graded Saturday-morning ensembles in Escondido, from a first-time Prelude Orchestra up to a full Symphony Orchestra that performs at the California Center for the Arts, Escondido.

season.value: 2026–27 performance season; tuition and registration due July 31, 2026

rehearsal.value: Saturdays 9:00 AM–noon (Prelude 9–11 AM) at Classical Academy High School — PLC, 130 Woodward Avenue, Escondido

commitment.value: Weekly Saturday rehearsals with roughly 3–4 performances per season; summer workshop required for Prelude–Wind ensembles and Labor Day retreat required for Symphonic and Symphony members

tuition.value: $725–$975 per season by ensemble (Prelude $725; Symphony Orchestra $975); $85 registration fee included; due July 31, 2026

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for North County families who want affordable Saturday-morning training with room to grow into a full symphony.

ensembles.name: Prelude Orchestra
ensembles.desc: First ensemble experience for string players with about a year of playing; weekly 2-hour rehearsals and three performances per season.

ensembles.name: Intermezzo Orchestra
ensembles.desc: String orchestra reinforcing fundamentals for players with 2+ years of experience.

ensembles.name: Intermediate Orchestra
ensembles.desc: String orchestra for players with 3+ years of experience exploring transcriptions of larger works.

ensembles.name: Chamber Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Advanced students drawn from the Intermediate Orchestra tackling higher-level string repertoire.

ensembles.name: Wind Ensemble
ensembles.desc: Woodwind, brass and percussion ensemble, primarily middle and high school students.

ensembles.name: Symphonic Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Full orchestra, primarily high school students; performs at least four times per season; Labor Day retreat required.

ensembles.name: Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Flagship full symphony playing original literature (Beethoven, Brahms, Shostakovich); advanced high schoolers plus some college students; performs at the California Center for the Arts, Escondido.

auditions.instruments: All orchestral instruments — placement across all seven ensembles

window.value: July 18, 2026 is the last audition for the 2026–27 season

auditions.note: Open to new students and current students moving up; placement determined by audition in spring and summer.

sources.label: CYO home (ages 5–22, July 18 audition, Escondido rehearsal location)

organizations.name: Mainly Mozart Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Mainly Mozart's youth program rehearses Saturdays in Solana Beach, with ensembles from entry-level strings to an advanced full orchestra and the chance to work alongside the festival's professional All-Star players.

season.value: Season typically runs September through May; 2026/27 summer auditions open July 1–31, 2026

rehearsal.value: Saturdays at Calvary Lutheran Church, Solana Beach; times by ensemble (Suzuki Stars 8:30–9:15 AM through Advanced Orchestra 9:30 AM–12:30 PM)

commitment.value: Weekly Saturday rehearsals, at least two concerts per season; max 4 excused / 2 unexcused absences per semester; private lessons required above the Suzuki Stars level

tuition.value: $725–$900 per student per season, depending on ensemble placement

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for coastal North County families — one Saturday location, a beginner-friendly entry point, and professional musicians in the room.

ensembles.name: Amadeus Strings (Suzuki Stars / Mozart Comets)
ensembles.desc: Entry-level strings for students with little or no prior experience; no audition to start.

ensembles.name: Prelude Strings
ensembles.desc: Early string orchestra (violin, viola, cello, double bass).

ensembles.name: Intermediate Strings
ensembles.desc: String orchestra for developing players.

ensembles.name: Concert Band
ensembles.desc: Full wind, brass and percussion band; winds/brass/percussion in Intermediate Orchestra also play here.

ensembles.name: Intermediate Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Full orchestra including winds, brass, percussion, harp and strings.

ensembles.name: Advanced Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Top full orchestra, rehearsing Saturday mornings; performs alongside Mainly Mozart events.

auditions.instruments: Openings by ensemble: Prelude Strings (all strings), Intermediate Strings (viola, bass), Concert Band (all instruments), Intermediate Orchestra (viola, bass, winds, brass, percussion), Advanced Orchestra (bass, horn, trombone, percussion)

window.value: Summer auditions for the 2026/27 season open July 1–31, 2026 (deadline Friday, July 31, 2026)

auditions.note: All placement auditions by online video submission.
2026/27 beginner enrollment is live on the site; no audition required for the entry-level ensemble.

sources.label: MMYO home (2026/27 auditions open July 1–31; ensembles and openings)
FAQ (tuition, financial aid, rehearsal times, season, expectations)
Who We Are (history since 1995, ensembles and instruments incl. harp)

organizations.name: East County Youth Symphony
organizations.blurb: A tuition-free East County program where young beginners start in the Pre-Orchestra Division and advanced players join a stipend-supported pre-professional orchestra, with two big concerts a year at the Joan B. Kroc Theatre.

season.value: Spring term January–May and fall term August–December (Pre-Orchestra Division)

rehearsal.value: Advanced Division: Fridays 6:00–8:50 PM at 7997 Paradise Valley Road, San Diego; Pre-Orchestra Division: Saturdays, one hour between 8:00 AM–12:30 PM at 2300 Bancroft Drive (Spring Valley)

commitment.value: All students perform twice a year at the Joan B. Kroc Theatre, plus weekly rehearsals

tuition.value: Free — tuition-free participation is the organization's mission

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if cost is a barrier — everything is tuition-free, from a child's first ensemble classes to the advanced orchestra.

ensembles.name: Pre-Orchestra Division
ensembles.desc: Tuition-free six-year training program: first-year classes (ages 6–10, open to all beginners) cover violin, small ensemble, piano and theory; continuing classes (ages 8–14) by audition.

ensembles.name: Advanced Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Pre-professional orchestra for advanced players, many pursuing college music degrees; by audition or invitation; each player receives a $100–$500 stipend per semester.

performances.title: Fall 2026 Showcase — world premiere of Vonn Vanier's 'Symphonic Suite'
performances.venue: Joan B. Kroc Theater, 6611 University Ave, San Diego
performances.program: Pre-Orchestra Division Fall 2026 Showcase; Advanced Orchestra world premiere of Vonn Vanier's Symphonic Suite, dedicated to the 250th anniversary of the USA

auditions.instruments: Pre-Orchestra Division (strings focus); beginners are interviewed in groups — no instrument playing required

window.value: Applications accepted year-round; term deadlines September 10 (fall) and February 10 (spring)

auditions.note: Age determined as of September 1 (fall term) or February 1 (spring term); in-person in Spring Valley or online via Zoom/Skype.

window.value: New students accepted all year round

auditions.note: By audition or invitation; 10 minutes of contrasting repertoire plus possible sight-reading; results usually same day (up to 3 weeks for competitive seats).

sources.label: Events (May 13 and November 18, 2026 concerts at Kroc Theater; venues)

organizations.name: New West Symphony
organizations.blurb: Ventura County's professional orchestra, led by artistic and music director Michael Christie, performing its Masterpiece Series in Thousand Oaks (BAPAC) and Camarillo (RCPAC). Its education wing runs student concerts, a traveling Music Van, and the tuition-free Laby Harmony Project in Ventura.

season.value: 2026 Masterpiece Series (calendar-year 2026 season celebrating the U.S. semiquincentennial)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want to hear a professional orchestra close to home, with family and student ticket pricing available.

ensembles.name: New West Symphony
ensembles.desc: Professional symphony orchestra performing in Thousand Oaks and Camarillo, with tenured members drawn from LA's film-recording scene.

performances.title: Beethoven & Copland
performances.program: Francesco Lecce-Chong conducts; with Pacific Festival Ballet and Los Robles Children's Choir. Repeats Sun Oct 4 at 3 PM in Camarillo (RCPAC).

performances.title: Symphony Goes to Cirque
performances.program: Michael Christie conducts; with Troupe Vertigo. Repeats Sun Nov 8 at 3 PM in Camarillo (RCPAC).

performances.title: Too Hot To Handel
performances.program: Michael Christie conducts; New West Symphony Chorus; Alfreda Burke, soprano; Rodrick Dixon, tenor. Repeats Sun Dec 6 at 3 PM in Camarillo (RCPAC).

sources.label: Orchestra Positions (last posting: Section Cello, Oct 2024)

organizations.name: Lyceum Oaks Youth Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Formerly the Conejo Valley Youth Orchestra, LOYO is a six-ensemble youth orchestra program entering its 66th season, rehearsing Mondays in Newbury Park with satellite campuses in Simi Valley and Ventura. Students climb a clear ladder from beginning strings and band to an advanced Symphony Orchestra and the invitation-only Virtuosi chamber orchestra.

season.value: 2026–27 season (LOYO's 66th); season begins September 11, 2026

rehearsal.value: Mondays at Godspeak Church, 320 Via Las Brisas, Newbury Park; ensemble blocks between 3:45 and 9:00 PM (Youth Strings 3:45–4:30, Preparatory 4:45–6:15, Symphony 6:30–8:15, Virtuosi 8:15–9:00)

commitment.value: Two-semester season of weekly Monday rehearsals (Sept 14, 2026 – Jun 4, 2027); Gala concerts and their dress rehearsals are mandatory

tuition.value: $1,000 per season or $700 per single semester (payment plans available; $20 discount for single September payment)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a structured, multi-level orchestra ladder in the Conejo Valley with clear advancement from first ensemble to pre-college level.

ensembles.name: Youth Strings
ensembles.desc: Junior string orchestra of 40–80 musicians where most string players begin (about 1–4 years of experience).

ensembles.name: Youth Band
ensembles.desc: Junior wind, brass, and percussion ensemble of 20–50 musicians for players in their first years.

ensembles.name: Flore Ensemble
ensembles.desc: Invitation-only string ensemble of 12–24 players bridging Youth Strings and Preparatory Orchestra.

ensembles.name: Preparatory Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Full symphonic orchestra of 60–80 intermediate string, wind, and brass players.

ensembles.name: Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Flagship full symphony of 70–90 advanced players tackling major repertoire from Brahms to Mussorgsky.

ensembles.name: Virtuosi Ensemble
ensembles.desc: Invitation-only chamber orchestra of Symphony principals; performs mostly conductorless with its own Dialogues concert series and concert stipends.

performances.title: Dialogues Messiah
performances.program: Handel's Messiah with the Virtuosi Ensemble and partner choirs

performances.title: Winter Gala
performances.program: All six LOYO ensembles (4:00 PM younger ensembles; 7:30 PM Virtuosi and Symphony Orchestra)

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion, saxophone (Youth Strings / Youth Band)

window.value: Registration open now; live auditions July 31 and August 1, 7, 8; standard registration closes August 3; make-up/sit-in auditions and late registration through September 11 (2026–27 season)

auditions.note: Entry-level musicians may request a sit-in audition instead of a live audition. Registration fee $20–$60 rises with later registration.

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion (Preparatory Orchestra, Symphony Orchestra, Virtuosi placement)

window.value: Registration open now; live auditions July 31 and August 1, 7, 8; standard registration closes August 3; make-up auditions September 11 (2026–27 season)

auditions.note: All applicants, new and returning, play an in-person audition: scale, solo excerpt, and orchestral excerpt. Placement is merit-based with no age or grade requirements.

sources.label: Schedule and tuition (2026–27 calendar, concerts, scholarships)

organizations.name: Bakersfield Symphony Orchestra
organizations.blurb: Kern County's professional orchestra, enriching Bakersfield since 1932 and performing its season at the Dignity Health Theater downtown. Education programs like Young People's Concerts and BSO NEXT serve students across the county.

season.value: 2026/27 season — 'A Season in Motion' (season tickets on sale now)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want live symphonic music in downtown Bakersfield, from the classics to holiday and mariachi nights.

ensembles.name: Bakersfield Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Professional symphony performing at the Dignity Health Theater; auditions are held blind behind a screen.

performances.title: Rhythm & Fire
performances.program: Bartók, Dvořák, and Ligeti; Jackson Snead performs Sarasate's Zigeunerweisen

performances.title: Music in Color
performances.program: Sibelius; Anastasiya Poff in 'The River'; Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition

performances.title: Home for the Holidays
performances.program: Holiday favorites and carols with Bakersfield College Combined Choirs and Bakersfield Master Chorale

sources.label: Auditions (blind audition policy; no current postings)

organizations.name: Bakersfield Youth Symphony Orchestra
organizations.blurb: An audition-only youth orchestra affiliated with the Bakersfield Symphony, open to students from 7th grade through age 19. Members rehearse Sunday evenings at CSU Bakersfield under music director Dr. Leo Sakomoto and perform three concerts a year.

season.value: 2026–27 season (calendar published; auditions September 12, 2026)

rehearsal.value: Sundays 5:30–8:00 PM at CSU Bakersfield (except three-day weekends)

commitment.value: Attendance at all rehearsals and concerts is mandatory for members

tuition.value: $275 per year ($25 application fee applies toward tuition; financial aid available based on need)

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if your middle or high schooler is ready for a step up from school ensembles and can commit to Sunday evening rehearsals.

ensembles.name: Bakersfield Youth Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Single audition-only ensemble of highly motivated students performing high-level orchestral repertoire, from 7th grade through first-year undergraduates.

window.value: Applications due September 4, 2026 ($10 late fee after); auditions September 12, 2026 at the CSUB Music Building, 9001 Stockdale Hwy

auditions.note: $25 non-refundable application fee. Assigned scales, excerpts, and sight-reading per instrument; students must be enrolled and in good standing in their school instrumental ensemble where available.

sources.label: Bakersfield Symphony home (BYSO affiliation; June 2026 BYSO season-finale mention)

organizations.name: San Bernardino Symphony Youth Orchestras
organizations.blurb: The San Bernardino Symphony's youth program fields two full orchestras — the Symphony Youth Orchestra for high schoolers and the Overture Orchestra for middle schoolers — with Sunday rehearsals, fall and spring concerts, and chances to share the stage with the professional symphony.

season.value: 2025–2026 season (fall and spring semesters; full calendar posted)

rehearsal.value: SBSYO: Sundays 2:00–5:00 PM at San Bernardino Valley College (North Hall 164); SBSOO: Sundays 3:00–4:30 PM at Wilmer Amina Carter High School

commitment.value: Weekly Sunday rehearsals September–May with fall and spring concerts; attendance policy per program handbook

tuition.value: 2025–26: SBSYO $200/semester ($400/year, or $350 if paid in full by Sept 7); SBSOO $100/semester ($200/year, or $175 in full); prorated for mid-year joins

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if your middle or high schooler wants a full-orchestra experience on a modest budget, with a path onto the professional symphony's stage.

ensembles.name: San Bernardino Symphony Youth Orchestra (SBSYO)
ensembles.desc: Full orchestra for ages 14–18 (grades 9–12); rehearses Sundays at San Bernardino Valley College and performs at the SBVC Auditorium and with the San Bernardino Symphony.

ensembles.name: San Bernardino Symphony Overture Orchestra (SBSOO)
ensembles.desc: Full orchestra for ages 11–14 (grades 6–9); rehearses Sundays at Wilmer Amina Carter High School. 9th graders are placed in SBSOO or SBSYO by audition.

auditions.instruments: Strings, woodwinds, brass, percussion, harp, keyboard (SBSYO)
auditions.note: 2025–26 auditions were held Aug 24–Sep 5, 2025; 2026–27 dates not yet announced as of 2026-07-06. Registration and video-audition forms stay posted online, and mid-year joins are possible with prorated tuition.

auditions.instruments: Strings, woodwinds, brass, percussion, harp, keyboard (SBSOO)
auditions.note: 2025–26 auditions were held Aug 24–Sep 5, 2025; 2026–27 dates not yet announced as of 2026-07-06. Registration and video-audition forms stay posted online.

sources.label: SBSYO/SBSOO program page — mission, tuition, calendar, rehearsals, auditions, financial aid

organizations.name: Riverside Arts Academy
organizations.blurb: Riverside's nonprofit music academy runs a full ladder of ensembles — from no-audition beginner strings through the auditioned Riverside Youth Orchestra, which rehearses Sunday evenings at UC Riverside — and full or partial need-based scholarships are available.

season.value: 2026–27 season; registration open

rehearsal.value: Riverside Youth Orchestra: Sundays 6:00–9:00 PM at UC Riverside; Overture Orchestra and beginner strings: Saturday mornings at the Arlanza Community Center, Riverside

commitment.value: Rehearsal attendance mandatory; up to 2 absences per semester (excluding dress rehearsals and concerts) under the RYO policy

tuition.value: Riverside Youth Orchestra: $1,200 per school year (3 units) before financial aid, payable in one sum or monthly; beginner strings classes are 1.5 units

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want one program that can carry a child from first beginner-strings lessons to an auditioned full youth orchestra without leaving Riverside.

ensembles.name: Riverside Youth Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Flagship auditioned full orchestra for grades 6–12 with 2+ years of experience; Sundays 6:00–9:00 PM at UC Riverside, with instrument-specific sectionals; repertoire spans the classical canon to film and video game music.

ensembles.name: Overture Orchestra
ensembles.desc: New auditioned intermediate full orchestra (strings, winds, brass, percussion) for grades 6–12; Saturdays 9:00 AM–12:00 PM at Arlanza Community Center.

ensembles.name: Strings II
ensembles.desc: Auditioned string orchestra (violin, viola, cello, bass) for grades 3–8; Saturday mornings at Arlanza Community Center.

ensembles.name: Strings I
ensembles.desc: No-audition entry strings class for grades 2–6; Saturday mornings at Arlanza Community Center.

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion (2+ years experience for RYO)

window.value: Registration for the 2026–27 season is open; RYO placement is by audition with posted excerpts and scales

auditions.note: Overture Orchestra and Strings II also require auditions; Strings I is no-audition. Students not placed in RYO are recommended to another RAA class.

sources.label: Riverside Youth Orchestra page — tuition, schedule, audition materials, attendance policy

organizations.name: Temecula Valley Youth Symphony
organizations.blurb: The Temecula Valley Symphony's youth division for student musicians in southwest Riverside County, with tiered ensembles from beginning strings to a full symphonic orchestra, weekly Monday-night rehearsals, and coaching from professional musicians.

season.value: Fall and spring semesters plus a summer session

rehearsal.value: Weekly Monday night rehearsals

commitment.value: Weekly rehearsals; 3–4 concerts per season plus community recital and chamber opportunities

organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want a youth orchestra close to home in the Temecula–Murrieta area rather than driving to Riverside or San Diego.

ensembles.name: Symphonic Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Top full-orchestra tier of the youth program.

ensembles.name: Concert Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Intermediate orchestra between beginning strings and the symphonic orchestra.

ensembles.name: Debut Strings
ensembles.desc: Entry-level string ensemble.

auditions.instruments: Strings, winds, brass, percussion — all ensemble levels

window.value: Rolling — audition any time during the school year before a scheduled rehearsal; video auditions accepted

auditions.note: All students re-audition at the start of each season (repertoire piece plus a scale; snare/mallet skills for percussion). The apply page still shows the 2025–26 cycle (spring session ended May 18, 2026) — confirm 2026–27 dates with the manager before relying on them.

sources.label: Youth Symphony program page (read via rendered browser — site requires JavaScript)
Apply & Audition (read via rendered browser; page shows 2025–26 cycle)

organizations.name: Redlands Symphony
organizations.blurb: A professional orchestra that has entertained the Inland Empire since 1950, performing its series at Memorial Chapel on the University of Redlands campus; 2026–27 is its 75th anniversary season under Music Director & Conductor Deanna Tham.

season.value: 2026–2027 season — 75th Anniversary Season

commitment.value: Professional orchestra; musicians are represented by AFM Local 47 and must be members in good standing to perform

organizations.goodFit: Good fit for families who want professional concerts in an intimate campus hall close to home; not a student ensemble.

ensembles.name: Redlands Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Professional symphony orchestra performing its subscription series at Memorial Chapel, University of Redlands.

performances.title: Shostakovich's Fifth
performances.program: Deanna Tham conducts — Derrick Skye 'Prisms, Cycles, Leaps'; Melanie Bonis 'Trois femmes de légende'; Shostakovich Symphony No. 5

performances.title: Pulcinella Suite
performances.program: Oswald Huỳnh 'Then, as if Breathing, the sea swelled beneath us'; Ibert Concertino da camera (Madelyn Olsen, alto saxophone); Stravinsky Pulcinella Suite

performances.title: Gulda Cello Concerto
performances.program: Gabrielli Fanfare; R. Strauss Suite in B-flat; Gulda Concerto for Cello and Wind Orchestra (Nathan Chan, cello)

window.value: No auditions currently scheduled; résumés accepted by email for the substitute list

auditions.note: Musicians must be AFM Local 47 members in good standing to perform.

organizations.name: San Bernardino Symphony Orchestra
organizations.blurb: The Inland Empire's professional symphony, led by Music Director Anthony Parnther, presenting a year-round series of orchestral, holiday, and jazz programming — and running its own two-tier youth orchestra program.
organizations.goodFit: Good fit if you want big-stage professional concerts in the heart of the Inland Empire — and a symphony family your young musician can grow into.

ensembles.name: San Bernardino Symphony Orchestra
ensembles.desc: Professional orchestra led by Music Director Anthony Parnther; its education wing fields the SBSYO and SBSOO youth orchestras (listed separately).

performances.title: This is Jazz
performances.program: The Josh Nelson Jazz Ensemble — works by Mulgrew Miller, Michel Petrucciani, Mike Wofford, Kenny Kirkland, George Cables, and Josh Nelson
